results.summary: Fresh signup reached Session with trial access. Private was available, Cloud was disabled for trial, setup CTA was visible, and no model cache/download appeared before user action.
Tab 1 stayed recording. Tab 2 showed Active session in another tab and stayed data-recording=false.
In-app Analytics navigation showed a confirmation dialog; accepting it stopped/saved and showed the saved session in Analytics.
Hard navigation to /analytics during Private recording lost the partial session and logged React Maximum update depth exceeded.
Five short Private Record/Stop cycles recovered to READY with no zombie recording, but emitted four React Maximum update depth exceeded warnings.
pnpm test:edge passed 11 files / 73 steps. Focused frontend hardening suite passed 72/72 across PDF, LiveRecordingCard, SignUpPage, AuthProvider, and SessionPage rendering/UI.

Focused Vitest entitlement proof passed 5 files / 76 tests across subscription tiers, transcription policy, session lifecycle, LiveRecordingCard, and free-plan support. Trial/private and Cloud gating logic are covered at unit/integration level.
pnpm run rc:dast:local built the test bundle and passed 18/18 Playwright checks covering primary journey, user features/PDF, entitlement, error states, and analytics truth.

A direct user-features Playwright command was excluded from product verdict because it did not build/serve frontend/dist first and failed with ENOENT for frontend/dist/index.html. Correct gate is pnpm run rc:dast:local, which passed.

pnpm run rc:dast:live stopped before browser execution because required live proof inputs were absent: BASE_URL, Supabase URL/anon/service-role keys, Free/Pro test credentials, and STRIPE_WEBHOOK_SECRET. This is not app-path proof and cannot count as live release evidence until env is supplied.

pnpm run rc:gate:1:product ran full ci:local. Code quality passed, E2E passed 33/33, CI report recorded no unit failure names and 100% passing rate, but final status failed because Lighthouse returned NO_FCP.

Lighthouse failed with NO_FCP. Manual preview reproduction at http://127.0.0.1:4173 showed the built test-mode app never painted: loader stayed visible, data-app-visible-ready=false, and console threw 'Mock auth is not available from the runtime app. Use the centralized E2E test harness or create real test users through the test-user workflow.' The release gate's Lighthouse path is incompatible with current test-mode preview/mock-auth guard.

pnpm run rc:gate:2:sast passed on current main. It completed quality/lint/typecheck/no-eslint-disable, frontend provider-secret scan, production-hardening scan, Edge tests 11 files / 73 steps, and focused Vitest 8 files / 78 tests.

pnpm run rc:gate:4:sca fails current main on critical vitest <4.1.0 advisory GHSA-5xrq-8626-4rwp: 'When Vitest UI server is listening, arbitrary file can be read and executed.' Audit reports 82 findings total: 5 low, 35 moderate, 41 high, 1 critical.

pnpm run rc:gate:5:ux passed current main 14/14 after a valid unsandboxed E2E server start. The sandboxed preview-bind attempt failed with EPERM and was rejected as invalid evidence, not a product failure.

devRequests.request: Fix hard-navigation/reload behavior while recording so users either cannot leave, or their partial recording is saved/recovered, with no React max-depth warning.
Root-cause rapid Private start/stop render-loop warnings and add a regression for five short cycles with zero max-depth warnings and no zombie recording state.

Fix the Lighthouse/product-gate preview path so rc:gate:1 can paint and audit the app without bypassing environment discipline. Either serve through the centralized E2E harness/mock-auth bridge or make the LHCI config use a valid preview route that does not throw before first paint.

Resolve the critical Vitest advisory in pnpm audit by upgrading/pinning the Vitest stack to a patched version or removing/isolating vulnerable Vitest UI exposure; rerun pnpm run rc:gate:4:sca and the relevant unit/front-end suites.
